chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمَرْأَةِ يَمْنَعُهَا زَوْجُهَا مِنْ حَجَّةِ الْإِسْلَامِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 44 – The woman who is forbidden by her husband from performing the Hajj of Al-Islam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/173)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/173)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( صلوات الله عليه ) عَنِ الْمَرْأَةِ الْحُرَّةِ تَحُجُّ إِلَى مَكَّةَ بِغَيْرِ وَلِيٍّ فَقَالَ لَا بَأْسَ تَخْرُجُ مَعَ قَوْمٍ ثِقَاتٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Muawiya who said,
‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the free woman going for Hajj to Makkah without a guardian, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem with her going out with a reliable group’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Mu‘awiyah who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a woman who is free if she can perform al-Hajj in Makkah. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful if she goes with trustworthy people.’”
